entry.title: Sap Inside Track Bangalore - 17th Feb Key Highlights
entry.content:
Dear Community Friends,

Hope you are doing good!

As part of the core content team organizing Sap Inside Track Bangalore on 17 February 2024, I am here to share the highlights of the event.

We saw an immense footprint of this sap inside the track right here in Bangalore.

Here, you see the overall feedback.

 

Top Event Feedback.

  1. In the event "AI and genAI," I particularly appreciate the focus on artificial intelligence (AI) and its intersection with generative AI (genAI). These topics are at the forefront of technological innovation and have significant implications across various industries. Exploring advancements, applications, and ethical considerations in AI and genAI can provide valuable insights into the future of technology and its impact on society. Additionally, the event likely offers opportunities for learning, networking, and collaboration with experts and enthusiasts in these fields, making it a compelling platform for knowledge exchange and professional growth.
  2. I had great time to know about SAP BTP, S/4HANA, SAP BTP & Other Products with AI, new cloud innovations, SAP Cloud ALM stall, SAP Datasphere, Integrations, SAP Build Low-code/No-code, etc. The community really helped me to know about the new innovations at SAP Labs to make us learn, market, implement for our customers.
  3. The hand's on and Track 4 sessions were phenomenal. As a Graduate Student, I had the opportunity to explore a lot in SAP. One specific thing I loved about the event was all about co-ordination and how happy it felt to be a part of the event.
  4. In this event we're getting so much learning material from experts.

Session Feedback

 

 

Based On the above result, below are the top  five liked session

  1. Harnessing the power of Resource Scheduling in S/4 Hana.
  2. Side-by-Side Extensibility with SAP Build Apps: Create Service Order with Appointment using Map View.
  3. Concur strategy.
  4. SAP Cloud ALM: A Comprehensive Overview and demo into Dynamic Alerting powered by AI
  5. Two-Tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: Taking Your Business to New Heights

 

Below are the three most adorable feedbacks for the session.

  1. Dharani's insights reveal a multifaceted approach to leveraging AI-driven analytics. Retailers are utilizing advanced algorithms to analyze diverse data sources, including transaction histories, browsing patterns, and demographic information, to create nuanced customer profiles. Dharani's examples underscore the transformative impact of AI-driven analytics on decision-making.
  2. The session featuring Ajit and Aryan focused on elucidating the transformative potential of the SAP Business Technology Platform in fostering adaptability for digital transformation. Through real-world examples and insightful analysis, they underscored the platform's role in enabling organizations to develop flexible and resilient applications capable of navigating dynamic business landscapes. Attendees gained valuable insights into the importance of adaptability and agility in driving successful digital initiatives, with practical strategies for leveraging the platform's capabilities to stay ahead in today's rapidly evolving market environment.
  3. Thanks Syambabu for the wonderful insights into SAP Graph. Explaining GRaphQL is the cherry on cake.

Topics Of Interest For Next Sap Inside Track.

  1. Business AI.
  2. BTP(Business Technology Platform) & Side-by-Side Extension.
  3. Cloud Based Solution like S/4Hana Public Cloud, Sap Cloud ALM.

Areas of Improvement.

  1. Time to extend for Q & A sessions.
  2. It was overall good. Need some more hands on sessions like sap code jam, Unlock power of AI, Build Apps. Because as a developer I felt and belive more practical knowledge gives more understanding in technology.
  3. Business use case based session.

Gratitude

I sincerely thank Abhishek Chatterjee and Mahesh Pallavalli for the opportunity and their trust in me to be included in the core team. 

I am thankful to my leadership Ashwini Ashok Naik and Arun Rachegowda, for allowing me to go beyond the boundaries of my day-to-day activities. I also express my deep gratitude toward Sindhu Gangadharan, whose leadership and strategic insights were crucial in expanding the SAP Community's reach in this region to new heights.

entry.title: Credit Horizon for deliveries
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Could anyone with credit management experience provide his/her thoughts?

Customer is applying a 2 step credit check:

1/ Age oldest open item < 30 days

2/ Dynamic credit limit check with horizon

of 0 days

Why a credit horizon of 0 days?

Well it often occurs that customers place sales orders for a whole year so to avoid an exponential increase in the credit exposure a credit horizon of 0 days is taken into consideration when calculating the open sales order commitment. This horizon can standard be achived with the dynamic check.

However, deliveries are created shortly after the creation of the sales order. The reason is that the Request for Transport (RFT), a document, is created from this delivery and communicated towards the transporter (forwarder) for freight planning purposes.

In credit management of SAP, all deliveries are taken in consideration for the credit exposure (no credit horizon is applied). This means that the credit exposure includes all open delivery amounts to check against the limit resulting in unjustified credit blocks. The customer’s credit limit is quickly surpassed due to the explosion in the total calculated credit exposure. The aim is to apply a credit horizon to deliveries with material availability date > current date

How could this be achieved without implementing any Badis? Is it possible standard after all ?

For the moment the option to write custom logic in Badi FSCM_COMMITMENT_UPDATE_DELVRY is on the table to ‘recalculate’ the open delivery amount before checking against the credit limit.

Looking forward to your thoughts

Mounir B

entry.title: Re: SAP Logon online language switcher
entry.content:
I had the same issue as @florianwolf and tried to change the tcode as per @aamelin1's suggestion but could not find a solution. Then I chose to follow a different path for the same goal. The sample code below works as a charm:

REPORT zlang.

DATA: gv_comm(80) TYPE c,
      gv_syst(80) TYPE c,
      gv_dest     TYPE msxxlist-name,
      gs_rfcsi    TYPE rfcsi.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME.
PARAMETERS p_spras TYPE spras OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

END-OF-SELECTION.

  CALL FUNCTION 'RFC_SYSTEM_INFO'
    IMPORTING
      rfcsi_export = gs_rfcsi.

  SET LOCALE LANGUAGE p_spras.

  gv_dest = gs_rfcsi-rfcdest.

  CALL FUNCTION 'TH_REMOTE_TRANSACTION'
    EXPORTING
      tcode        = space
      dest         = gv_dest
    IMPORTING
      comm_message = gv_comm
      syst_message = gv_syst.

entry.title: Celebrating International Pi Day
entry.content:
Do you like numbers? Mathematics? Are you a lover of formulas? Maybe you are passionate about mathematical constants? If you have an interest in numbers, you'll surely be interested in International Pi Day. Today, Pi is celebrated - the coolest constants. It is one of the first, if not the first constant we learn.

With this special constant you can calculate the area of a circle (A = π r²), or perimeter (circumference) using 2πr. Pi itself is derived from the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter. The number produced is 3.14 or 22/7, and has been around for about 4000 years.

It has captured poets, mathematicians, artists, historians, and with people memorizing and reciting the number of digits for world records, which is currently at 70,000 digits. Pi is also evident in nature, in fact it can be also be calculated from measuring winding rivers. For me Pi is a magical constant.

So back to the first question, what are your favorite numbers/formulas/constants?

And to throw in an old pun - what is your favorite pie? (mine's apple 😊).

Looking forward to hearing from you in the comments below.

entry.title: Re: Principal Propogation, Event Mesh setup and User Cont...
entry.content:
Hi @nileshpuranik,

That topic on authentication is not related to your ERP backend authentication. It is rather the authentication that would happen when you call your service implemented with CAPM. 

Therefore, it will not make any difference whether you create a new instance of that object of not. It will simply not be used when you call your ERP end-point.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Error in navigating to the association in CAPM entity with @restrict annotation with custom attr
entry.content:
Dear all,

In a simple CAPM application, I have a 2 entities defined with parent and child relationship, salesOrder and salesOrderItems. The entities are from an external service oData V4. There is no persistence layer so no 'db' module in the application.

In the handler method of the entities, I am connecting to the external service and passing the query as it is.

In the xs-security.json file, I have defined certain role collections and roles with custom attribute customerId. I would like to grant access and show data for the customer based on the custom attribute assigned in the role, so that the user can only see the data based on the customerId assigned to him/her via the role.

On the header entity, I have used the restriction like this

@(restrict: [
{
grant: ['*'],
where: '$user.customerId = SoldToParty'
}

Without this restriction, everything works fine.

But when I add this restriction, the List report shows the data correctly ( filtered sales order as per the customerId in the role assigned to the user ) but the moment, I select a record and try to go to the object page in the fiori elements app, it gives me an error.

Basically it tries to carry the same filter of customerId while making call to the child entity which is not required.

Any advice how can i overcome this issue ? 

Many thanks in advance!

Br

Nilesh

entry.title: SAP Business ByDesign bank statement with return and unification
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a question about how to resolve a case and I wanted to know if anyone could help me.

I explain, I have three ids of outgoing payments with the following amounts:
- ID 5 - $500.00
- ID 6 - $250.00
- ID 7 - $250.00
When I receive my bank statement, it turns out that the bank accumulated the three IDs into a single outgoing amount, so I only have one outgoing transaction of $1,000.00. 
In addition to that, one of the payments was returned to me by the bank and I found out when I viewed my bank statement. 
So on my bank statement I have the following transactions:
- Outgoing - -$1.000,00
- Incoming - $250,00
When i do the payment allocation i have no idea how to separate the amount of the $250,00 that the bank returned, to mark it as a refund and then assign it to the $250,00 incoming.

I may have an idea of how to solve this but first I would like to know what it's your opinion

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 2
entry.content:
Why not creating the CloudEvent in Groovy if we want to use Integration Suite for that?

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.util.HashMap
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat

def Message processData(Message message) {

    //read properties
        def properties = message.getProperties()
        specversion = properties.get("specversion")
        type = properties.get("type")
        source = properties.get("source")
        id = properties.get("id")

    //create timestamp
        def date = new Date()
        def sdf = new SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")

    //read data from body

        def data = message.getBody(String)

    //create CloudEvent

        message.setBody("{\r\n\"specversion\": \"" + specversion +"\",\r\n\"type\": \""+ type +"\",\r\n\"source\": \""+ source +"\",\r\n\"id\": \""+ id +"\",\r\n\"time\": \""+ sdf.format(date) +"\",\r\n\"datacontenttype\": \"application/json\",\r\n\"data\":" + data + "}")
    return message
    
}

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF Index table always stores first occurrence of...
entry.content:
Hi @lei_su, Thank you again for your help. I maintained the previous Single Index table in SAP Application Interface Framework > Error Handling > Define Namespace-Specific Features. And Multi Index table under, SAP Application Interface Framework > Error Handling > Define Interface-Specific Features.
Now no error while saving.
The result of this is that it stores a single entries in Single Index table ( Z copy of AIF/STD_IDX_TBL ) and Two/multiple entries in Multi Index table ( Z copy of  /AIF/MIDX_TEMPL ) based on different values we have in IDOC. 
In single Index table, not all Key fields values are not getting stored. And in Multi Index table, It looks like it will store multiple entries of only one Key fields. In my case I have 5 key fields of which 3 can have multiple values for which we want to search based on Qualifier.
But this isn't helping in searching failures based on Key Values

entry.title: Automatic packing for outbound delivery using Unified Package Builder- Customer Connect - 281072
entry.content:
Automatic Packing in Outbound:

With S/4 HANA 2022 & higher version, it is now possible to conduct 'Automatic Packing' against Outbound delivery in EWM work center. The automatic packing functionality was available for Inbound process receiving against the Inbound delivery, same concept is extended to outbound process using more robust packaging algorithm through Unified package builder( UPB)

Long Packing Process-Earlier it was a tedious job to select every product/item manually and create HU’s to pack them in EWM work center,

Current State -To make this process easier for the customers, auto pack functionality is introduced- where the customer can simply choose the product to be packed and click on “Auto pack” button which will identify the appropriate packaging materials of the products and create the handling units automatically.

Scope
• User can mark one or several stock items or a HU in the tree of the outbound work center
• The systems calls the Packing Instructions (PI) or Pack Specification (PS) via UPB for the directly marked stock and for the stock in a marked HU.
• If a PI or PS has been found the systems creates the Hus accordingly.
• If no PI or PS has been found the stock remains where it is.

In below example, a delivery is already picked and confirmed via Pick HU( Non Homogeneous HU).

Perform the Auto Packing Activity. Ensure to enter the default storage Bin.

The system automatically packs using the packaging specification defined for condition 0OBD for product combination. UPB engine, sequence and packaging specification gets determined.

The product gets packed into 2 Shipping HUs as per the condition record 0OBD.

More robust packaging specification can be create using Product, Product Ship to, Product with Consolidation Group, Product W/O consolidation group.

2 Shipping HUs are generated which is result of automatic packing.

 

SAP has enabled a new method 'AUTOPACK' in Packing super class /SCWM/CL_WM_PACKING. This new method( Public) can be leveraged to built a Z report which could conduct automatic packing based on Picked ODO. You can use class /SCWM/CL_WM_PACKING method  passing parameters  IV_BIN (storage bin- Mandatory), ODO IV_PRDO, WC,IV_WORKSTATION. Please read all the import parameter in the class what are optional and mandatory

This Packing super class can also be used in Custom RF packing process for automatic Packing. In the code, we always create the Shipping HUs only for picked deliveries. There are also few exception related to serial numbers and Catch weight products.

This functionality is enabled from S/4HANA 2022 & higher version. The note 3301323 -Automatic packing for outbound delivery  introduces a check in FM /SCWM/CALL_PACK_OBDL to trigger automatic packing based on below condition.

Configuration:

UPB setting: Doc type OUTB: Extended Warehouse Management-->Goods Issue Process-->Outbound Delivery-->Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process

UPB Setting:

Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Unified Package Building-->Define Unified Package Building Profile

Exceptions & Fulfillment for Automatic Packing

1) Only Picked delivery is relevant for Automatic Packing. /SCWM/PRDO will fail to trigger packing if it_stock and iv_prdo are initial .

2) Only picked stocks without assignment of serial numbers (C) Serial Number are allowed for automatic packing

3) Only picked stock´s without using catch weight are allowed for automatic packing 

Thanks for reading. All tests have been performed in S/4 HANA 2022 Embedded EWM sandbox system.

entry.title: "Reimagining Customer Experience in the Utility Industry: Insights Sharing at the Energy & Utilities
entry.content:
Facing incessant pressures, demanding customers, and pursuing sustainability goals represents a day in the life of utility companies.  At the SAP we have observed that certain themes have remained consistent in the industry over the past decade. While our primary focus lies on enhancing the Customer Experience (CX) aspects, our responsibilities extend far beyond that. It is essential to adopt a comprehensive outlook on industry processes to genuinely deliver outstanding experiences.

I am excited to share my expertise at the Energy & Utilities Conference in Seville this April, where I have the privilege of leading the Customer Experience track. This event offers an amazing opportunity to learn from our customers and partners through engaging presentations and demonstrations. Please join us at the SAP booth for further discussions — I look forward to engaging with you in Seville.

Recently, I had an enlightening encounter with a high-ranking executive of a leading European utility company. Among the diverse array of queries, he threw my way, one stood out: “From your perspective, what is the biggest CX-related misconception among utility companies?”

The question wasn’t the novelty; instead, it was the shift in the approach of energy companies showcasing a desire for proactive engagement.

Today, every organization, utilities or otherwise, claims to have a customer-centric strategy. These are often well-intentioned declarations with little to no grounding in employee mindsets, goals, or concrete operational processes. True customer focus is not merely about implementing a CRM or any other CX solutions. Rather, it necessitates the alignment of processes, personnel, and technological resources in an orchestrated effort to craft a comprehensive strategy that places the customer squarely at the center of all activities. To illustrate my point, let's consider the 360° customer view concept. It has been around since the inception of CRM , yet the misunderstanding persists. It is not about everyone having a complete view of the customer, but it is about tailoring it to individual requirements. For instance, marketing and customer service teams will need different insights.

In the quest to create a customer-focused solution, the importance of the human element and their daily goals should not be overshadowed. To make sense of all these facets, we should not forget the linchpin— the customer. They have diverse priorities, their journeys vary, and their expectations differ.

Starting with a customer-centric approach is commendable but scaling and adapting it to align with various dimensions: people, processes, technology, and customers, is imperative. This is the biggest misstep I've noted among several utility companies.

The good news is that it's not an impasse. As a matter of fact, this misstep provides an excellent pivot point, and we at SAP are ready to offer support to all energy companies across these dimensions.

entry.title: SAP BTP - Monitoring Usage and Consumption Costs in Your Global Account
entry.content:
Monitoring Usage and Consumption Costs in Your SAP BTP Global Account

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) offers a flexible pricing model that caters to your business needs. You can choose between a consumption-based commercial model, where you pay for what you use, or a subscription-based model for predictable costs. 

Consumption-based commercial models can be a great way to optimize your costs and ensure that you're only paying for the resources that you need.

In order to effectively manage your costs, it's important to be able to monitor your usage and consumption. The SAP BTP global account provides a Costs and Usage page that can help you with this.

What is the Costs and Usage Page?

The Costs and Usage page is a central location where you can view information about your global account's usage and consumption of SAP BTP services. This information can help you to:

  • Understand your current usage patterns
  • Identify trends in your usage
  • Forecast your future costs
  • Make informed decisions about your SAP BTP usage

How to Access the Costs and Usage Page

The method to access cost and usage information depends on your global account's commercial model:

  • Consumption-Based Model: Navigate to the Costs and Usage page in the SAP BTP cockpit. This page offers a centralized view of your global account's SAP BTP service usage and consumption costs.

  • Subscription-Based Model: Access the Usage page within the SAP BTP cockpit. This page provides information on your service usage but excludes cost details.

Understanding the Costs and Usage Page

The Costs and Usage page provides valuable insights for optimizing your SAP BTP resource allocation and expenses. Here's a breakdown of the key information displayed:

  • Time Period: The time frame covered by the usage data.
  • SAP BTP Services: Lists all SAP BTP services utilized within your global account.
  • Service Plan Consumption: Details the amount consumed for each service plan.
  • Cost: Indicates the associated cost for each service plan.

Conclusion

By leveraging the Costs and Usage page, you can:

  • Identify Usage Trends: Analyze historical data to recognize usage patterns and predict future requirements.
  • Make Informed Decisions: Tailor your resource allocation based on usage trends to optimize costs.
  • Avoid Bill Surprises: Monitor your consumption to stay within budget expectations.

By effectively monitoring usage and consumption costs, you can ensure you're getting the most value out of your SAP BTP investment.

I hope this blog post has been helpful. Please let me know if you have any questions.

entry.title: Re: Starting SAP Companion for desktop from Another Server
entry.content:
Hi,

No, the approach that you mention below is only necessary if you want to deploy the SAP Companion for Desktop content from another web server, not Enable Now Manager.  I suspect that the vast majority of customers using this capability will deploy the content from Enable Now Manager, not an external Tomcat server.

If you don't have a specific reason for deploying the Enable Now content from Manager, you should:

1. Publish the SAP Companion for Desktop content in Producer and synchronize the details to Manager
2. In Manager, click the down arrow beside All Content (top, right corner) and select Published.  This will display the Published version of your content.  Your SAP Companion content must be published
3. Click on your SAP Companion for Desktop folder
4. Click the SAP Companion link (top, right corner).  This will download a file called navigator.senl

The navigator.senl file must be deployed to end user machines and started.  When it is opened on an end user machine, the content of the file is passed to the Enable Now Launcher application, which must also be installed on every end user machine, and the Launcher uses the configuration in the navigator.senl to open SAP Companion for Desktop and point it to the content in Manager.

There are details in the Desktop Components Installation Guide that is available from the Enable Now site on help.sap.com that describe the process of deploying SAP Companion for Desktop from Manager.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Customizing of GOS (Generic Object Service) working f...
entry.content:
Generally we use Harmonized Document Management in S/4HANA which covers you for GOS, DMS, and other document frameworks. Many SAP Fiori apps are now using this approach via the F1243 Attachment Service app. This is the current documentation link for the latest release of HDM: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/36802406aebb4b96b1598246e1d316ee/bc275e43f1b043cc8f97710d5de64e97.html?version=2023.001  

This is the link to the Attachment Service app - you can see in Related Apps many of the apps using this service. https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1243')/S28OP 

Essentially HDM solves the old GUI problem of competing document frameworks where attachments showed up in different places at different times which was very confusing for users. Now they can all be seen by using HDM.

entry.title: Missing purpose string in Info.plist
entry.content:
I am currently facing an issue delivering my IOS Build in Transporter:

ITMS-90683: Missing purpose string in Info.plist - Your app’s code references one or more APIs that access sensitive user data, or the app has one or more entitlements that permit such access. The Info.plist file for the “RNVApp.app” bundle should contain a NSSiriUsageDescription key with a user-facing purpose string explaining clearly and completely why your app needs the data. If you’re using external libraries or SDKs, they may reference APIs that require a purpose string. While your app might not use these APIs, a purpose string is still required.

I have attempted a numerous amount of fixes such as including that key in the plist file and then resigning the IPA file however I always get numerous errors in which they are identifying the files as invalid and that it has not been signed correctly.

Can this be added to the user strings section when configuring the build as other strings have?

  • Parent and child are single-select fields.

entry.title: Re: Export Job Posting from SF to Corporate Site
entry.content:
Hi Carolina,

 

The recommended way to provide job postings to a third party is through the Recruiting Marketing (RMK) tool, which can automatically distribute jobs to all relevant locations. 

It is possible to utilize web services to build your own XML extract from our tool and pass it to a third party, or to purchase a Boomi license and contract Professional Services time to have this built custom. 

There is a standard pre-packaged XML export for posted jobs; this is not configurable, the contents will NOT be changed upon request and in the medium/long term this will probably be deprecated, so while it is technically available today it is not advisable to rely on this feature as a long-term solution.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.05
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.05 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q2 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.399 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Expiration of file-repository backups | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Exploration
    • Variable Dialog Enhancements | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • SAP Analytics Cloud add-in for Microsoft Excel
      • Recent List in Open Dialog
      • Custom Function: GETVARIABLES
      • Support for SAP BW and SAP S/4HANA live models
  • Story Design
    • Lite Viewer Incremental Updates
  • Story Extensibility
    • Enhance Exporting all Table Content to Excel

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Expiration of file-repository backups

Model backups stored in the file repository older than 90 days will expire. Expired files will be available in the deleted folder in the file repository, where they are retained for 30 days by default before permanently being deleted from the system. The automated cleanup of backup files reduces housekeeping efforts. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data Exploration

 

Variable Dialog Enhancements

Enhancements have been made to the variable dialog for improved function and usability: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • New compact and responsive design
  • A new settings panel with the option to configure the text presentation of variable values (ID,

description, or ID and description together)

  • Improved interaction with and readability of multi select variables
  • The single select now appears as editable text in the input field

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

SAP Analytics Cloud add-in for Microsoft Excel

 

Recent List in Open Dialog

The Select Data Source dialog does now include the recent list Recent Data Sources containing the last 10 data sources you used on this tenant and device. The data sources can be from different connection types.

Additionally, the connections are now grouped by type, for example, SAP Datasphere or SAP S/4HANA systems, where you can select the corresponding data sources.

This feature is available to all customers as of Feb 28, 2024.

 

Custom Function: GETVARIABLES

Introducing new custom function SAP.GETVARIBALES. This custom function can be used to retrieve variable values of a data source.

Parameters:

  • Model ID / Data source
  • Variables (optional)
  • Display variable (0=ID, 1=Description) (optional)
  • Display value (0=ID, 1=Description) (optional)
  • Layout (separate cells, single cells etc.) (optional)
  • Orientation (0=vertical, 1=horizontal) (optional)

This feature is available to all customers as of Feb 28, 2024.

 
Support for SAP BW and SAP S/4HANA live models

In addition to live connection, the SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Excel now supports live models in SAP BW and SAP S/4HANA.

This feature is available to all customers as of Feb 28, 2024.

 

Story Design

 

Lite Viewer Incremental Updates

Lite Viewer now supports the following features and will NOT re-direct the Viewer to the Full Application:

  • Launch Data Analyzer from Chart and Table
  • Geographical Visualization – Heatmap Layer
  • Export to PDF with Geographical Visualization will no longer display a Ghost Widget within the Export

 

Story Extensibility

 

Enhance Exporting all Table Content to Excel

In addition to Point of View, users can now select “All” as the scope to export all the data of a table to an XLSX file.

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: SAP Enable Now Information Sessions (European Friendly Times)
entry.content:
This post contains the registration details for the free SAP Enable Now information sessions, delivered on European-friendly times.  This post is continually updated with new session details so please check back on a regular basis.  Finally, if you are interested in an SAP Enable Now information session on a topic that is not listed below, leave a comment on the post.  We will monitor the comments and consider all suggestions.
Session times are specified in Central European Time (CET).

Future Sessions (European friendly times)

TopicDescriptionDate and TimeDurationLevelSession Registration
Publishing SCORM Content using SAP Enable NowThis session introduces the concepts necessary to successfully publish SAP Enable Now content in the SCORM format. This session will cover:
  • An overview of the concept of SCORM
  • A discussion on the Workarea and content settings impacting the publishing of content in the SCORM format
  • An overview of the approach to structuring content to be published as SCORM
  • The process of publishing content as SCORM
  • The loading and testing of SCORM content into an LMS.
21 February 2024
9:30am CET		2hrsIntroductorySession registration
Introduction to SAP Enable Now AdministrationThis session discusses key activities associated with administering an SAP Enable Now system. This session will cover:
  • An overview of SAP Enable Now administration activities
  • An overview of SAP Enable Now server settings impacting an entire environment
  • An overview and demonstration of the key activities associated with administering SAP Enable Now user records
  • An overview and demonstration of the key activities associated with managed SAP Enable Now user Roles and Workareas
  • An overview and demonstration of the task of cleaning up the Unsorted folder in Manager
  • An overview of SAP Enable Now housekeeping functionality.
28 February 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsIntroductorySession registration
Administering Content in SAP Enable Now ManagerThis session discusses key concepts associated with the administration of content in SAP Enable Now Manager. This session will cover:
  • The process of searching for and locating content in Manager
  • The process of defining the content structure in Manager
  • The process of assigning individual content objects to content authors in Manager
  • The process of assigning content to end users so that they can access the content through Manager
  • The process of modifying individual content objects in Manager
  • The process of publishing content in Manager
  • The process of cancelling an author’s control of a content object in Manager
  • The process of restoring previous versions of a content object in Manager
  • The process of deleting and restoring content objects in Manager
  • The process of accessing end user feedback about Enable Now content.
6 March 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsIntroductorySession registration
Accessing and Using Standard SAP ContentThis session discusses the process of obtaining access to standard content, created and maintained by SAP, and modifying and using that content. The topics covered in this session include:
  • An overview of SAP standard content
  • The benefits to using SAP standard content
  • The process for accessing SAP standard content
  • The process to modify and republish SAP standard content in SAP Enable Now.
20 March 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsIntroductorySession registration
Creating and Deploying Branded Content LibrariesThis session introduces the key concepts associated with creating a branded content library in SAP Enable Now. The topics covered in this session include:
  • An overview of SAP Enable Now library functionality
  • Details of settings and templates that can be used to control the library
  • Details about structuring library content to allow users to access content by browsing the content structure
  • Details about publishing content libraries directly from SAP Enable Now Manager.
27 March 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsIntroductorySession registration
Obtaining End User Feedback for SAP Enable Now ContentThis session discusses and demonstrates the content feedback capability within SAP Enable Now. The session covers:
  • The activities necessary to turn on the end user feedback functionality
  • The options to configure the standard feedback functionality
  • The options for end users to provide content feedback
  • Reviewing end user feedback
  • The options available for SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion customers to provide feedback about SAP Enable Now solutions to the product team.
17 April 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
Creating and Editing Enable Now Simulations using ManagerThis session will discuss and demonstrate the Manager capability to record and edit Enable Now Simulations. The session includes:
  • An overview of the Manager Simulation recording and editing capability
  • The benefits and limitations of using Manager to create Simulations, rather than Producer or Instant Producer
  • The process of recording Simulations using Manager
  • An overview of the Simulation editing functionality available in Manager.
If you are planning to decentralise the creation of Simulations, using SMEs in the business to do the recordings, this session will definitely help you to understand your choices.		24 April 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
SAP Companion Overview and BenefitsThis session discusses and demonstrates the use of SAP Companion to increase user adoption of SAP systems. SAP Companion is the component of SAP Enable Now that provides SAP system users with continuous opportunities to learn, as they work in live systems. The session includes:
  • An overview of SAP Companion capability and its benefits
  • A demonstration of SAP Companion
  • A brief discussion on how to get started with SAP Companion.
If you are interested in exploring mechanisms to deliver a more proficient SAP workforce, this session will definitely be worthwhile.		1 May 2024
9:30am CET		1hrSolution OverviewSession registration
Introduction to Deploying SAP Companion for Non-SAP ApplicationsThis session will discuss and demonstrate the key concepts associated with deploying SAP Companion on non-SAP applications. The session includes:
  • An overview of the use of SAP Companion on non-SAP applications
  • An overview of the process used to integrate SAP Companion into a non-SAP application
  • A discussion on the prerequisites for using SAP Companion in a non-SAP application
  • A discussion and demonstration of the setup work that occurs in SAP Enable Now Manager
  • A discussion and demonstration of the setup of the browser extension, used to deliver the integration
  • A demonstration of SAP Companion working in a browser-based application.
Please note: There is a technical component to deploying SAP Companion on non-SAP applications, which includes the creation of Javascript. This session will provide an overview of the technical activities and discuss the resources available to support this work but no technical integration work will be demonstrated.		15 May 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
Using SAP Enable Now Advanced Book Page FunctionalityThis session will discuss and demonstrate SAP Enable Now Book Page functionality that can be used to create interactive and engaging eLearning content. The session covers:
  • Using book page object actions and links to create interactive content
  • Using shape effects to modify the formatting of shapes, e.g. to create buttons
  • Using object animations to transition objects onto and off of the page
  • Using Controls, such as the Timer and Collector collects to develop interactive content.
If you are interested in developing engaging, interactive eLearning content using SAP Enable Now, this session will be useful.		22 May 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
Delivering Enable Now Content through Manager Learner ViewThis session will discuss and demonstrate the capability to deploy content through the SAP Enable Now Manager Learner View. The session includes:
  • An overview of the capability to deploy content through Manager Learner View
  • The settings necessary to track the completion of content accessed through Learner View
  • The creation of Assignment records to assign content to end users
  • Accessing and running content through the Learner View tab
  • The impact of Business Roles on the content distributed through Learner View
  • Reporting on the consumption of content accessed through Learner View.
29 May 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
SAP Enable Now 2406 New Release OverviewThis session discusses and demonstrates key enhancements that are to be deployed with the 2406 (June 2024) release of SAP Enable Now. Although the exact enhancements to be deployed through this release are not yet published, it is expected that the release could include:
  • Enhancements to SAP Companion functionality
  • Enhancements to the capability to create and manage Simulation recording through Manager
  • Further integrations to other SAP applications, e.g. CALM.
Please note: the exact content of this session will ultimately be determined by the enhancements deployed through the 2406 release of SAP Enable Now.		5 June 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
Reviewing SAP Enable Now Content (including the Use of the Enable Now Workflow Approver)This session will discuss and demonstrate the options for reviewing SAP Enable Now content, including the use of the Enable Now Workflow Approver. The session covers:
  • The prerequisites to allow users to review unpublished Enable Now content
  • The options for reviewing Enable Now content
  • The creation and use of SAP Enable Now Workflows to manage the status of individual content objects
  • Enable Now reports to track the status of content objects
  • The use of the Enable Now Workflow Approver to review and provide feedback about individual content objects.
19 June 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
Controlling Access to SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion Content ObjectsThis session introduces key concepts that can be used to control access to SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion content by end users. The session covers:
  • An introduction to SAP Enable Now capability that is used to control access to content
  • A discussion and demonstration of the process of limiting access to SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion content, using Business Roles
  • A discussion and demonstration of the process of limiting access to SAP Companion content, using SAP Companion Conditions.
If you are interested in targeting groups of users with specific content but don't want to create many Workareas, this session will introduce some very useful new functionality.		26 June 2024
9:30am CET		1.5hrsSolution OverviewSession registration
Creating and Using SAP Enable Now Book Page TemplatesThis session introduces the basic concepts associated with the creation of Books and Book Pages in Enable Now. The session covers: - The importance and process of creating content with reference to templates - Basic book page creation concepts, such as adding objects to a page, changing the properties of the objects and aligning objects - Concepts associated with structuring books and book pages - The process of re-purposing PowerPoint content in Enable Now - Publishing books and book pages through Enable Now Manager.
  • The benefits of using Enable Now templates

entry.title: SAP Enable Now Information Sessions - Previous Webinar Recordings
entry.content:
This post contains links to previous SAP Enable Now information session webinar recordings.  This post will be continually updated with new recordings so please check back on a regular basis.

Previous Session Recordings

TopicDescriptionDurationLevelSession Recording
SAP Companion Overview and BenefitsThis session focusses on the use of SAP Companion, a component of SAP Enable Now, and its use to drive greater user adoption of your SAP browser-based applications, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba. The session covers:
  • An overview of SAP Companion capability and its benefits
  • A demonstration of SAP Companion
  • A Q&A session to allow audience members to obtain a deeper understanding of the solution.
If you are interested in exploring mechanisms to deliver a more proficient SAP workforce, don't miss this session.		1hrSolution OverviewSession recording
Introduction to SAP CompanionThis session discusses the use of SAP Companion to support end user adoption of SAP browser-based applications, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba.  This session will cover:
  • An overview of SAP Companion
  • SAP Companion setup and configuration (an overview)
  • Deploying content through SAP Companion.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Historical Performance Data
entry.content: We are also in the need of publishing historical performance ratings to user's Employee profile. Please let us know the options available.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Historical Performance Data
entry.content: Have you succeeded in setting up historical data through MDF objects?

entry.title: Re: Export Book as PDF
entry.content:
The missing .PDF or .DOCX document might be due to the bug the SAP Enable Now Product Support team is investigating. In short, the documents won't generate for some customers with the document labelling (public, internal, confidential, etc) active in their MS Office suite. 

 Unfortunately, there is no solution for now, just workarounds:

  1. Try generating the document in MS Word Format, then convert it manually to PDF
  2. Select both .doc and .pdf formats when generating single documents.
  3. Disable the document labelling in your MS Word or the template you utilize in SAP Enable Now.

Anton

entry.title: Re: The amount format changes based on destination country
entry.content:
Hello...

Could you pls explain a little further?
The currency is the same for both cases...
The currency is EUR.
It is only the format that gets changed depending on the destination country while using the write statement...

entry.title: Hi all… Is it possible to transfer stock between two valuation type?
Re: SAP Developer Challenge - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (Week 4)
entry.content:
@nicoschoenteich wrote: We have already come a long way with our application since week 1 of the challenge. We have a solid data model, load initial data and can track golf rounds, but still there is one central component missing - the golf players. Wouldn't it be nice to track who played a round of golf? The simplest approach here would be to create a new entity in our data model, but I think we should use this as an opportunity to learn how to consume remote services. The remote service that we are going to consume exposes the API of this SAP Community Groups platform, because the players will be you folks! We will integrate an remote service that lists everyone who completed week 1 of this challenge successfully, and these community members will be the "golf players".

 

Integrating the SAP Community Groups platform to enlist successful participants from week 1 as golf players enhances our application's functionality elegantly. By leveraging remote services, we honor our community's achievements and foster a sense of camaraderie. This integration not only enriches user experience but also showcases our commitment to embracing collaborative opportunities. With each stroke on the golf course, we'll celebrate the shared journey of our esteemed participants.

entry.title: Re: Font in layout of adobe form is not the one in the print preview
entry.content:
Hello... Thank you so much for this...
You were right... Seems like the font was removed from the ADS server. 
It was resolved by the BASIS team...

entry.title: Re: The amount format changes based on destination country
entry.content: use a TVARVC table to store destination country list and assign relevant Decimal format which will be utilised while invoice PDF is generated to do the format conversion as required.

entry.title: Re: Can interviewer rating displayed as percentage?
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The interview ratings will be displayed as an average of the competencies' ratings.

This cannot be displayed in percentage format.

 

Regards,

Nishitha

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Application Item node create button not visib...
entry.content: If you preview the direct option available in the binding, please update your Eclipse ADT to the latest version. It happened to me. Once Updated then it started working.

entry.title: Re: MD04 MRP
entry.content: Hello, I appreciate your feedback. I looked over the steps you wrote and they sounded reasonable, then I looked over the material master data and saw that there was data missing from the production revision. I fixed the production revision area. And the issue was resolved.
